channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump offering federal workers buyouts with about 8 months’ pay in effort to shrink government
item.description: The Trump administration announced Tuesday that it is offering buyouts to all federal employees who opt to leave their jobs by next week — an unprecedented move to shrink the U.S. government at breakneck speed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump order aims to end federal support for gender transitions for those under 19
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order aimed at cutting federal support for gender transitions for people under age 19, his latest move to roll back protections for transgender people across the country.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Texas senators fast-track school voucher bill for full vote
item.description: The Senate could vote on the proposal as soon as next week. Democrats on Tuesday criticized the bill saying it fails to prioritize the state’s neediest children.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Jaden Edison, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump offering federal workers buyouts with about 8 months' pay in effort to shrink government
item.description: The Trump administration says it is offering buyouts to all federal employees who opt to leave their jobs by next week — an unprecedented move to shrink the U.S. government at breakneck speed.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sherman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Leavitt on offense at first briefing, stressing Trump will deport 'heinous' illegal immigrant criminals
item.description: Leavitt said that the Trump administration is removing "heinous individuals" from American communities every day to crack down on illegal immigration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bipartisan senators demand Trump explanation for IG firings
item.description: The leaders of the Senate Judiciary Committee sent a rare bipartisan letter to President Trump demanding an explanation of his firing of 17 inspectors general in one night.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt
item.description: Karoline Leavitt, 27, is the youngest person to hold the position of press secretary since Ron Ziegler, who worked as President Nixon’s top press official.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Six active duty service members file first lawsuit challenging Trump's transgender troop ban
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Six transgender active duty service members and two former service members who seek re-enlistment on Tuesday filed the first lawsuit challenging President Donald Trump’s executive order that calls for revising policy on transgender troops and probably sets the stage for banning the
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gary Peters will not run for reelection, opening key Senate seat
item.description: The 66-year-old’s unexpected decision Tuesday to step aside after just two terms comes as a surprise.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump administration freeze on federal grants and loans
item.description: A federal judge has temporarily blocked a push from President Donald Trump to pause federal funding while his administration conducts an across-the-board ideological review to uproot progressive initiatives.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: News for Trump: Denmark's last envoy to Greenland says U.K. may have first dibs
item.description: Denmark's last minister for Greenland says Britain once claimed right of first refusal over the territory — because of its proximity to Canada, which Britain once ruled.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer, MPR News

item.title: Trump addresses House Republicans at Florida retreat: 5 takeaways
item.description: DORAL, Fla. — President Trump floated running for a third term, commented on a new Chinese artificial intelligence app, and urged House Republicans to unify and quickly advance his legislative priorities in a speech to the lawmakers at their annual policy retreat.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Johnson opens door to linking voter ID to California disaster aid after Trump demand
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is keeping the door open to conditioning California disaster aid on the state enacting voter identification laws, days after President Trump said he wanted to link the two priorities.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: NJ congressman launches plan to tackle rising meat and egg prices
item.description: FORT LEE, N.J. (PIX11) -- Food prices in the coming year are expected to continue to increase, according to the Department of Agriculture. It's unlikely to be the sharp rise we saw in the past few years, but certain products such as eggs and meats continue to rise more than most other groceries.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: DOJ fires prosecutors who worked on Trump criminal cases
item.description: The move impacted at least a dozen prosecutors who worked both on Trump’s election interference case as well as another for improperly retaining records at Mar-a-Lago.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Bishop who enraged Trump says she was speaking from faith
item.description: Trump, who sat alongside JD Vance, appeared annoyed, looking away from Budde as she spoke directly to him.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Senate tees up more confirmations as House GOP talks Trump agenda
item.description: (The Hill) — The Senate is set to churn through more of President Donald Trump’s cabinet nominees this week, with a handful of his controversial picks set to field questions during their confirmation hearings. Tulsi Gabbard, Trump’s pick to be director of national intelligence; Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota lawmakers hit a reset after Supreme Court ruling, but stalemate persists
item.description: Like a scene from the movie “Groundhog Day,” life in the Minnesota House continued to repeat itself Monday — a cycle of gaveled to order, no quorum, no business, gaveled to adjournment. The Senate also saw some controversy.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: DeSantis faces pushback by fellow Republicans on his call for an immigration session
item.description: Republican leaders in the state legislature say they support President Donald Trump's pledge to combat illegal immigration but want to deal with it and other issues identified by DeSantis on their schedule, not his.
item.dc:creator: Greg Allen, MPR News

item.title: NJ bill looks to eliminate hurdle for student service members
item.description: FLORHAM PARK, N.J. (PIX11) - From studying to serving: that was National Guard veteran Frank "Doc" Schupp's life in the late 2000s. He was called to deploy to Iraq at the end of his last semester of his junior year at Kean University, in the midst of prepping for finals.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Trump pumps brakes on Day 1 tariffs
item.description: President Donald Trump pumped the brakes on his plan to deliver wide-ranging tariffs as soon as he took office, slow-tracking and toning down changes to the U.S. trade system that figured as a centerpiece of his campaign.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, WFLA, NBC

item.title: GOP congressman wants bishop 'added to deportation list' after Trump prayer service
item.description: Rep. Mike Collins heavily criticized statements made by the Right Rev. Mariann Budde on Tuesday at the inaugural prayer service held for President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Trump popularity higher than throughout most of first term: Poll
item.description: The poll found Trump with a 47 percent approval rating, higher than almost the entirety of his first term, though still below the majority support that most other presidents have enjoyed at the start of their administrations.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5
